description: Following revelations of multiple extramarital affairs in November 2009, major sponsors including Accenture, AT&T, and Gatorade faced public pressure to drop Tiger Woods. This case captures the rapid erosion of brand affinity when a primary celebrity endorser's private conduct conflicts with their 'wholesome' brand marketing image.

key_events.description: National Enquirer breaks the story of infidelity.
Accenture becomes the first major sponsor to terminate their endorsement contract.
Woods announces an 'indefinite break' from professional golf.
Woods issues a televised apology confirming full departure from public spotlight.

calibration_notes: Excellent for modeling the 'rapid collapse' trajectory where initial brand trust buffers the decline before shifting to permanent reputational loss.
